title: prunella vulgaris var. lilacina
intro: Prunella vulgaris var. lilacina, also known as the American foxglove or purple foxglove, is a plant species within the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to North America. The leaves and flowers of this plant are used in herbal medicine, which has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These medicinal properties contribute to various health benefits, including relieving fever, reducing cough and cold symptoms, and supporting the immune system. Prunella vulgaris var. lilacina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate respiratory issues.
